AuditEventType.description: Discriminated event types recorded in the audit trail.

AuditEvent.description: A single audit event recorded in the forge-ts audit trail.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event occurred.

user.description: OS username of the actor (falls back to "unknown").

reason.description: Mandatory for lock/unlock/bypass events; optional otherwise.

ReadAuditOptions.description: Options for reading the audit log.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return.

eventType.description: Filter to a single event type.

BypassConfig.description: Configuration for the bypass budget system.

dailyBudget.description: Maximum number of bypasses allowed per calendar day. Default: 3

durationHours.description: Duration in hours before a bypass automatically expires. Default: 24

BypassRecord.description: A single bypass record stored in `.forge-bypass.json`.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the bypass was created.

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the bypass expires.

reason.description: Mandatory justification for why the bypass was created.

rule.description: Specific rule code bypassed (e.g., "E009"), or "all" for a blanket bypass.

user.description: OS username of the actor who created the bypass.

Visibility.description: Visibility levels for exported symbols. Derived from TSDoc release tags (public, beta, internal).

ForgeSymbol.description: A single extracted and annotated symbol from the TypeScript AST.

name.description: The declared name of the symbol.

kind.description: The syntactic kind of the symbol.

visibility.description: Resolved visibility from TSDoc release tags.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the source file.

signature.description: Human-readable type signature of the symbol.

children.description: Child symbols (e.g., class members, enum values).

exported.description: Whether this symbol is part of the public module exports.

RuleSeverity.description: Severity level for an individual enforcement rule. - `"error"` — violation fails the build. - `"warn"`  — violation is reported but does not fail the build. - `"off"`   — rule is disabled entirely.

EnforceRules.description: Per-rule severity configuration for the TSDoc enforcer. Each key corresponds to one of the E001–E007 rule codes.

\"require-summary\".description: E001: Exported symbol missing TSDoc summary.

\"require-tsdoc-syntax\".description: W006: TSDoc syntax parse error (invalid tag, malformed block, etc.).

\"require-remarks\".description: E013: Exported function/class is missing a

\"require-default-value\".description: E014: Optional property with default is missing defaultValue.

\"require-see\".description: W005: Symbol references other symbols via  but has no

\"require-release-tag\".description: E016: Exported symbol is missing a release tag (public, beta, internal).

\"require-fresh-guides\".description: W007: Guide FORGE:AUTO section references a symbol that no longer exists or has changed.

\"require-guide-coverage\".description: W008: Exported public symbol is not mentioned in any guide page.

\"require-inheritdoc-source\".description: W009:  references a symbol that does not exist.

\"require-no-ts-ignore\".description: E019: Non-test file contains ts-ignore or ts-expect-error directive.

\"require-no-any-in-api\".description: E020: Exported symbol has `any` in its public API signature.

\"require-fresh-link-text\".description: W012:  display text appears stale relative to target summary.

ForgeConfig.description: Full configuration for a forge-ts run. Loaded from forge-ts.config.ts or the "forge-ts" key in package.json.

tsconfig.description: Path to the tsconfig.json to compile against.

enforce.description: Enforce TSDoc on all public exports.

skill.description: Skill package generation settings. Custom sections here are merged into the generated SKILL.md, allowing projects to inject workflow knowledge, domain gotchas, and other context that cannot be derived from symbols alone.

bypass.description: Bypass budget configuration for temporary rule overrides.

project.description: Project metadata — auto-detected from package.json if not provided.

ForgeResult.description: The result of a forge-ts compilation pass.

success.description: Whether the run succeeded without errors.

symbols.description: All symbols extracted during this run.

errors.description: Errors that caused or would cause failure.

duration.description: Wall-clock duration of the run in milliseconds.

writtenFiles.description: Absolute paths of files written during this run (populated by gen).

ForgeError.description: A diagnostic error produced during a forge-ts run.

filePath.description: Absolute path of the file where the error occurred.

suggestedFix.description: Suggested fix for the agent — exact TSDoc block to add.

symbolName.description: The symbol name that needs fixing.

symbolKind.description: The symbol kind (function, class, interface, etc.).

ForgeWarning.description: A diagnostic warning produced during a forge-ts run.

filePath.description: Absolute path of the file where the warning occurred.

ForgeLockManifest.description: Manifest stored in `.forge-lock.json`. Captures a point-in-time snapshot of the project's forge-ts configuration so that future runs can detect when settings have been weakened.

version.description: Schema version of the lock manifest.

lockedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the lock was created.

lockedBy.description: Identifier of the user or agent that created the lock.

LockViolation.description: A single violation found when comparing current config against the lock.

field.description: Dot-path of the config field that changed (e.g., "rules.require-summary").

locked.description: The value stored in the lock file.

current.description: The current value in the live config.

type.description: The data type of the schema (e.g., "string", "number", "object", "array").

format.description: A format hint for the data type (e.g., "int32", "date-time", "email", "uuid").

description.description: A human-readable description of the schema's purpose or constraints.

properties.description: Property definitions for object-type schemas. Maps each property name to its schema.

required.description: List of property names that must be present on the object.

items.description: Schema definition for the elements of an array-type schema. Required when `type` is "array".

additionalProperties.description: Controls whether additional properties are allowed (`true`/`false`) or defines their schema.

enum.description: Restricts the value to one of the listed constants.

oneOf.description: Validates the value against exactly one of the listed sub-schemas.

allOf.description: Validates the value against all of the listed sub-schemas (intersection).

anyOf.description: Validates the value against at least one of the listed sub-schemas.

nullable.description: Indicates that the value may be `null` in addition to its declared type.

deprecated.description: Marks the schema as deprecated, signalling that it may be removed in a future version.

default.description: The default value to use when the property is absent.

$ref.description: A JSON Reference (`$ref`) pointing to another schema definition in the document.

title.description: The human-readable name of the API.

version.description: The version string for the API (e.g., "1.0.0").

description.description: A detailed description of the API, supporting CommonMark markdown.

summary.description: A short summary of the API, intended for display in tooling.

license.description: Licensing information for the exposed API, including name, URL, and SPDX identifier.

name.description: The name of the tag, used to group operations in the document.

description.description: An optional description of the tag, supporting CommonMark markdown.

summary.description: A short summary of the path item, intended for tooling display.

description.description: A detailed description of the path item, supporting CommonMark markdown.

get.description: The operation definition for HTTP GET requests to this path.

post.description: The operation definition for HTTP POST requests to this path.

put.description: The operation definition for HTTP PUT requests to this path.

delete.description: The operation definition for HTTP DELETE requests to this path.

patch.description: The operation definition for HTTP PATCH requests to this path.

options.description: The operation definition for HTTP OPTIONS requests to this path.

head.description: The operation definition for HTTP HEAD requests to this path.

trace.description: The operation definition for HTTP TRACE requests to this path.

query.description: The operation definition for HTTP QUERY requests to this path (OpenAPI 3.2 extension).

additionalOperations.description: Additional non-standard HTTP method operations keyed by method name.

operationId.description: A unique string identifier for the operation, used by tooling to reference it.

summary.description: A short, human-readable summary of what the operation does.

description.description: A detailed description of the operation's behaviour, supporting CommonMark markdown.

tags.description: A list of tag names that logically group this operation in documentation and tooling.

parameters.description: The list of parameters applicable to this operation.

responses.description: The possible responses returned by this operation, keyed by HTTP status code or "default".

name.description: The name of the parameter, case-sensitive.

in.description: The location of the parameter: path, query, header, cookie, or querystring.

description.description: A human-readable description of the parameter's purpose, supporting CommonMark markdown.

required.description: Whether the parameter is mandatory. Required for `in: "path"` parameters.

schema.description: The schema defining the type and constraints of the parameter value.

deprecated.description: Marks the parameter as deprecated; clients should avoid using it.

contentType.description: The MIME type to use for encoding a specific property (e.g., "application/json").

headers.description: Additional headers to send alongside the encoded part, keyed by header name.

style.description: The serialization style for the encoded value (e.g., "form", "spaceDelimited").

explode.description: Whether arrays and objects should be exploded into separate query parameters.

allowReserved.description: Whether reserved characters in the encoded value should be allowed without percent-encoding.

schema.description: The schema defining the structure and type of the media type's payload.

encoding.description: Encoding information for specific properties of a `multipart` or `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` request body.

description.description: A required human-readable description of the response, supporting CommonMark markdown.

headers.description: HTTP headers returned with this response, keyed by header name.

content.description: The response body content, keyed by media type (e.g., "application/json").

openapi.description: The OpenAPI specification version this document conforms to. Must be "3.2.0".

$self.description: An optional self-referencing URL for this document, used for tooling and resolution.

info.description: Metadata about the API including title, version, and description.

paths.description: The available paths and their operations, keyed by path template (e.g., "/users/id").

components.description: Reusable schema and media type definitions shared across the document.

tags.description: A list of tags used to group operations, with optional descriptions.

SDKProperty.description: A single property extracted from an interface or class symbol.

type.description: The TypeScript type string of the property.

required.description: Whether the property is required (not optional).

SDKType.description: An SDK type descriptor extracted from the symbol graph.

properties.description: Extracted properties (for interfaces, classes) or values (for enums).

sourceFile.description: Absolute path to the source file.

ReferenceEntry.description: A single entry in the generated API reference.

children.description: Nested child symbols (class methods, interface properties, enum members).

CommandOutput.description: Typed result from a forge-ts command.

operation.description: Name of the command that produced this output (e.g., "check", "build").

errors.description: Structured errors produced by the command, if any.

warnings.description: Structured warnings produced by the command, if any.

duration.description: Wall-clock duration of the command in milliseconds.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the source file containing the error, if applicable.

line.description: 1-based line number of the error, if applicable.

column.description: 0-based column number of the error, if applicable.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the source file containing the warning, if applicable.

line.description: 1-based line number of the warning, if applicable.

column.description: 0-based column number of the warning, if applicable.

OutputFlags.description: Output format flags passed through from citty args.

json.description: Emit output as a LAFS JSON envelope instead of human-readable text.

human.description: Emit output as formatted human-readable text.

quiet.description: Suppress all output regardless of format.

success.description: Whether the audit log was read successfully.

GuideSource.description: The source heuristic that discovered a guide.

DiscoveredGuide.description: A guide discovered from the symbol graph by code analysis heuristics.

description.description: Short description of the guide's content.

symbols.description: The symbols that contribute content to this guide.

MdPhrasing.description: Union of all inline (phrasing) content types.

MdList.description: Block node: ordered or unordered list.

MdBlock.description: Union of all block content types.

FrontmatterResult.description: Result of parsing frontmatter from markdown/MDX content.

body.description: The body content without the frontmatter block.

data.description: The parsed frontmatter data as a key-value map.

stub.description: When true, this page is scaffolding intended for human/agent editing. Stub pages are created only on the first build and never overwritten, preserving manual edits across subsequent `forge-ts build` runs. Auto-generated pages (stub=false) are always regenerated from source.

SiteGeneratorOptions.description: Options controlling the doc site generator.

packageName.description: npm package name for install commands.

GeneratedFile.description: A file to write to disk during scaffolding or generation.

path.description: Relative path from the docs output directory.

stub.description: When true, this file is scaffolding intended for human/agent editing. Stub files are created only on the first build and never overwritten, preserving manual edits across subsequent `forge-ts build` runs. Callers should check this flag and skip writing if the file exists.

SSGStyleGuide.description: Style guide configuration for the SSG target.

supportsMdx.description: Whether the target supports MDX components.

requiresFrontmatter.description: Whether frontmatter is required on every page.

defaultImports.description: Component imports to add at top of MDX files (if supportsMdx).

codeBlockLanguage.description: Code block language for TypeScript examples.

ScaffoldManifest.description: Scaffold manifest describing what `init docs` creates.

target.description: The SSG target this manifest is for.

symbols.description: All symbols extracted from the project.

DevServerCommand.description: Command to start a local dev server for doc preview.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the binary.

url.description: The URL the dev server will be available at.

SSGAdapter.description: The central SSG adapter interface. Every doc platform provider implements this contract. One file per provider. No shared mutable state.

mdx.description: Whether to use MDX syntax (default: Markdown).

badge.description: Include a "Documented with forge-ts" badge above the API table.

SkillPackage.description: A generated skill package following the agentskills.io directory structure. Contains SKILL.md plus optional references and scripts files.

directoryName.description: The skill directory name (lowercase, hyphens only, max 64 chars).

files.description: Files to write inside the skill directory.

SSGConfigFile.description: A single generated SSG configuration file.

forceStubs.description: When true, overwrite stub pages even if they already exist on disk. Normally stub pages (concepts, guides, faq, contributing, changelog) are only created on the first build to preserve manual edits. Use this to reset stubs to their scaffolding state.

BuildStep.description: A single step in the build pipeline.

outputPath.description: Path to the primary output file produced by this step, if applicable.

duration.description: Wall-clock duration of this step in milliseconds.

errors.description: Errors produced by this step when status is "failed".

generatedFiles.description: Files written during the build — present at standard and full MVI levels.

BypassCreateResult.description: Typed result for the `bypass` command when creating a bypass.

success.description: Whether the bypass was successfully created.

bypass.description: The bypass record that was created.

remainingBudget.description: Number of remaining bypass slots for today after creation.
Number of remaining bypass slots for today.

expiredRemoved.description: Number of expired bypasses that were cleaned up.

DeprecatedUsage.description: A detected usage of a deprecated symbol.

sourcePackage.description: The package that exports the deprecated symbol.

consumingFile.description: The file importing the deprecated symbol.

FormatOptions.description: Options that control how `formatResults` renders its output.

colors.description: Emit ANSI colour escape sequences when `true`.

verbose.description: When `true`, include the symbol's type signature alongside each diagnostic so the reader has immediate context.

CheckFileError.description: A single error entry within a file group.

suggestedFix.description: Exact TSDoc block to add (present at full MVI or with --rule/--file filters).

CheckFileWarning.description: A single warning entry within a file group.

symbol.description: Symbol name that generated the warning.

CheckFileGroup.description: Errors and warnings grouped by file.

file.description: Absolute path to the source file.

CheckRuleCount.description: Error breakdown by rule code, sorted by count descending.

files.description: Number of unique files affected by this rule.

CheckTriage.description: Triage data for prioritizing fixes. Always present when the check has errors, bounded in size (~9 rules + top 20 files).

byRule.description: Error counts by rule, sorted descending.

fixOrder.description: Suggested fix order: rules sorted by fewest files affected first (quick wins).

hasMore.description: Whether more results exist beyond this page.

total.description: Total number of file groups (after filters).

success.description: Whether the check passed without errors.

summary.description: Aggregate counts — always present regardless of MVI level.

triage.description: Triage data for prioritizing fixes — present when errors  0 (except minimal).

byFile.description: Per-file breakdown — present at standard and full MVI levels, paginated.

page.description: Pagination metadata when byFile is paginated.

filters.description: Active filters applied to this result.

nextCommand.description: CLI command hint for the agent to run next.

InitProjectEnvironment.description: Detected project environment from Step 1 of the init flow.

typescriptVersion.description: TypeScript version from dependencies, or null if not found.

monorepo.description: Whether the project is a monorepo.

skipped.description: Files that already existed and were skipped.

scriptsAdded.description: Scripts that were added to package.json (empty if none were added).

DoctorCheckStatus.description: Status of a single doctor check.

DoctorCheckResult.description: Result of a single doctor check.

fixable.description: Whether this issue can be auto-fixed with --fix.

success.description: Whether all checks passed without errors.

fixed.description: Files that were fixed (only populated when --fix is used).

target.description: The SSG target that was scaffolded.

files.description: Relative paths of all files created.

HookManager.description: Detected hook manager in the project.

hookManager.description: The detected or chosen hook manager.

files.description: Relative paths of all files written.

success.description: Whether the lock was successfully created.

lockFile.description: Path to the lock file that was written.

lockedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the lock was created.

lockedBy.description: Identifier of who created the lock.

overwrote.description: Whether a previous lock file was overwritten.

success.description: Whether both check and build passed.

build.description: Result of the build step (absent if check failed and build was skipped).

skippedReason.description: If check failed, the reason build was skipped.

ExtractedExample.description: A single extracted `@example` block ready for test generation.

symbolName.description: The symbol this example belongs to.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the source file.

code.description: The raw code inside the fenced block.

index.description: Sequential index among examples for this symbol.

GeneratorOptions.description: Options for virtual test file generation.

cacheDir.description: Directory where virtual test files will be written.

path.description: Absolute path where the file will be written.

RunResult.description: Result of running the generated test files.

output.description: Combined stdout + stderr output from the test runner.

tests.description: Individual test results with name and status.

TestCaseResult.description: The result of a single test case.

name.description: The full test name as reported by the runner.

sourceFile.description: The source file this test was generated from, if determinable.

TestFailure.description: A single test failure entry, included at standard and full MVI levels.

symbol.description: Symbol name where the doctest failed.

file.description: Absolute path to the source file.

line.description: 1-based line number of the failing example.

summary.description: Aggregate counts — always present regardless of MVI level.

failures.description: Per-failure details — present at standard and full MVI levels.

previousLockedBy.description: Who originally locked the config, if known.

previousLockedAt.description: When the config was originally locked, if known.
